source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the cloud computing service provider is established in {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the infrastructure and assets of the cloud computing service provider, including those of its subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the service, are located in {TRUSTED_REGION} unless the {GOVERNMENT_CUSTOMER} explicitly requires otherwise.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the customer data, including metadata and telemetry data, that is processed, stored and transferred by the cloud computing service provider, and by the subcontractors, which are involved in the provision of the service, remain exclusively within {TRUSTED_REGION}, unless the {GOVERNMENT_CUSTOMER} explicitly requires otherwise and at any time, including before, during or after the configuration or use of the service.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: where the cloud computing service provider outsources the technical and operational support or assistance, including any subsequent sub-outsourcing arrangements, to third-party service providers outside of {TRUSTED_REGION}, the necessary legal, technical and organisational measures are implemented to ensure traceability, security and governance of those operations and those operations do not, in any way, compromise the operational autonomy of the cloud computing service provider.

needs_review_note: sov_domain choice (SOV-4 vs. SOV-5) remains an open judgment call — the criterion is about outsourcing arrangements to third-party providers, which could plausibly sit under SOV-5/supply_chain_services instead of SOV-4. [Layer: operations_personnel finalized, Phase 3 layer adjudication — the domain question is separate and stays open.]

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the cloud computing service provider demonstrates that the service complies with the state-of-the-art cybersecurity standards.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the cloud computing service provider provides full transparency around the use of subcontractors. The cloud computing service provider subjects subcontractors to due diligence, contractual obligations and ongoing oversight to meet Union legal obligations.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: Where the cloud computing service provider is subject to the control of a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, the cloud computing service provider guarantees that there are no existing laws and practices in that outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, demonstrated by independent sources, that require the cloud computing service provider to report information on software vulnerabilities to authorities of that outside {TRUSTED_REGION} prior to those vulnerabilities being known to have been exploited.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service are established in {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the infrastructure, assets, and personnel of the audited provider, including those of its subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the service are located in {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the customer data, including metadata and telemetry data, that is processed, stored and transferred by the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the service, remain exclusively within {TRUSTED_REGION}, unless the {GOVERNMENT_CUSTOMER} explicitly requires otherwise and at any time, including before, during or after the configuration or use of the service.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited service obtains a {TRUSTED_REGION} cybersecurity certificate of at least assurance level 'substantial' under a {TRUSTED_REGION} cybersecurity certification scheme covering cloud computing services, recognized by {NATION} or {TRUSTED_REGION} (source cites: Regulation (EU) 2019/881), provided that such a scheme has been established and is available to cloud computing service providers. Until the establishment of such a scheme, national cybersecurity certification schemes shall apply, where they exist. Where no {TRUSTED_REGION} or national cybersecurity certification schemes exist, the audited provider is to demonstrate that the service complies with the highest cybersecurity standards under applicable {TRUSTED_REGION} law.

generalization_note.en: R7 override: Regulation (EU) 2019/881 is a named EU legal instrument that does not generalize to a placeholder (a {NATION} may have no equivalent scheme, or a differently-numbered one). Reframed generically ("recognized by {NATION} or {TRUSTED_REGION}") with the instrument retained as a parenthetical source example, per rule R7.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the data generated by using the audited service are not used to train or fine-tune any AI system operated by a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, and are not transferred outside {TRUSTED_REGION} in any case.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: if the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service are subject to the control of a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, they demonstrate that the necessary legal, technical and organisational measures have been implemented to ensure that the: i. control of the outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or the legal entity established in a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} over the audited provider is not exercised in a manner that restrains or restricts the provider's ability to perform and deliver the service, imposes limitations on the infrastructure, assets, and personnel required for the service provision, or undermines the capabilities and standards necessary to perform the audited service; ii. access by a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or by an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION} to customer data is prevented; iii. possibility of disruption of the service continuity and/or the degradation of the service quality by a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION} is prevented; iv. control of the outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or the legal entity established in a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} over the audited provider is not exercised in a manner that obliges the audited provider to implement, enforce, give effect to, or comply with restrictive measures such as sanction regimes, embargoes, or any equivalent legal or administrative measures adopted by a outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, unless such measures are legitimate under the national laws of {NATION} or {TRUSTED_REGION} law.

needs_review_note: Third-country control/ownership test combines SOV-1 (strategic ownership/control, the defining concept — 'subject to the control of') and SOV-2 (jurisdictional exposure, the mechanism); tagged SOV-1 for consistency with ECSF's own 'Change of Control Risk' factor (csat-sov1-ecsf-02).

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the technical and operational support or assistance related to the audited service, including subsequent sub-outsourcing arrangements, are initiated and performed exclusively within {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited provider demonstrates that the following software supply chain measures are in place: i. a complete and up-to-date software bill of materials (SBOM) (source cites: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847, Article 3, point (39)), and a list of identified dependencies relevant to the provision of the service are documented and made available to the auditing organisation; ii. where software components (source cites: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847, Article 3, point (6)) or products are provided, owned, and licensed by an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, controls are implemented and documented to block any remote features that could materially tamper with or disrupt a device, system, or software (including during updates) and to ensure that the security-relevant components from software manufacturers outside {TRUSTED_REGION} (source cites: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847, Article 3, point (13)) are subject to source code audits, and have a documented migration plan in the event that the vendor fails or an entity outside {TRUSTED_REGION} imposes restrictions; iii. where the cloud computing service provider is subject to the control of an entity outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, the cloud computing service provider guarantees that there are no existing laws and practices in that jurisdiction, demonstrated by independent sources, that require the cloud computing service provider to report information on software vulnerabilities to authorities of that jurisdiction prior to those vulnerabilities being known to have been exploited.

generalization_note.en: R7 override: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 (the Cyber Resilience Act) is cited three times in the verbatim text to define SBOM, software component, and third-country manufacturer respectively; all three generalize to a parenthetical source citation per rule R7 rather than a placeholder substitution, since a {NATION} may have no equivalent instrument. Mechanical "a outside {TRUSTED_REGION}" phrasing from rules R5c/R5d was also tidied to "an entity outside {TRUSTED_REGION}" for readability while preserving the same substitutions.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: where software released under an open-source licence is used for the provision of the service, the audited provider demonstrates that it has implemented and documented the appropriate controls to prevent the use of any remote features or mechanisms that could be used to materially tamper with or disrupt a device, system, or software.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: to the extent that the audited provider provides its services globally and maintains a subsidiary in a outside {TRUSTED_REGION}, the audited provider has implemented the necessary measures to ensure and enforce the effective legal, technical and organisational separation between {TRUSTED_REGION} parent company and any such outside {TRUSTED_REGION} subsidiary.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service are established in {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the infrastructure, assets, and personnel of the audited provider, including those of the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the service, are located in {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the customer data, including metadata and telemetry data, that is processed, stored and transferred by the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the service, remain exclusively within {TRUSTED_REGION} unless the {GOVERNMENT_CUSTOMER} explicitly requires otherwise and at any time, including before, during or after the configuration or use of the service.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the personnel, including the personnel of the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service are {TRUSTED_REGION} citizens and where appropriate, the personnel must also have the necessary national security clearance issued by a {NATION} when handling classified information (source cites: Regulation (EU) 2021/697, Article 2, point (21)).

generalization_note.en: R7 override: Regulation (EU) 2021/697's definition of "classified information" generalizes to a parenthetical source citation per rule R7 rather than a placeholder substitution.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited service obtains a {TRUSTED_REGION} cybersecurity certificate of at least assurance level 'substantial' under a {TRUSTED_REGION} cybersecurity certification scheme covering cloud computing services, recognized by {NATION} or {TRUSTED_REGION} (source cites: Regulation (EU) 2019/881), provided that such a scheme has been established and is available to cloud computing service providers. Until the establishment of such a scheme, national cybersecurity certification schemes shall apply, where they exist. Where no {TRUSTED_REGION} or national cybersecurity certification schemes exist, the audited provider is to demonstrate that the service complies with the highest cybersecurity standards under applicable {TRUSTED_REGION} law.

generalization_note.en: R7 override: same reasoning as csat-sov7-cada-ua2-e (identical criterion text at UA-3).

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the data generated by using the audited service are not used to train or fine-tune any AI system operated by a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION} and are not transferred outside {TRUSTED_REGION} in any case.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service are not subject to the control of an entity outside {TRUSTED_REGION}. By way of derogation to this criterion, a cloud computing service provider and its subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service that are subject to the control of an entity outside {TRUSTED_REGION} may be audited for Union assurance level 3 where {NATION}'s competent authority has made a formal adequacy/allowlist decision for that jurisdiction (see this record's own needs_review_note on the Article 18/19 citation discrepancy in the source text). Where such a decision has been made, the audited provider and the subcontractors which are involved in the provision of the audited service must also demonstrate that the necessary legal, technical and organisational measures have been implemented to ensure that the: i. control of that jurisdiction or the legal entity established there over the audited provider is not exercised in a manner that restrains or restricts the provider's ability to perform and deliver the service, imposes limitations on the infrastructure, assets, and personnel required for the service provision, or undermines the capabilities and standards necessary to perform the audited service. The audited provider should allow for reasonable access to the code; ii. access by that jurisdiction or an entity established there to customer data is prevented; iii. possibility of disruption of the service continuity and/or the degradation of the service quality by that jurisdiction or an entity established there is prevented; iv. control of that jurisdiction or the legal entity established there over the audited provider is not exercised in a manner that obliges the audited provider to implement, enforce, give effect to, or comply with restrictive measures such as sanction regimes, embargoes, or any equivalent legal or administrative measures adopted by that jurisdiction, unless such measures are legitimate under the national laws of {NATION} or {TRUSTED_REGION} law.

needs_review_note: Third-country control/ownership test combines SOV-1 (strategic ownership/control, the defining concept — 'subject to the control of') and SOV-2 (jurisdictional exposure, the mechanism); tagged SOV-1 for consistency with ECSF's own 'Change of Control Risk' factor (csat-sov1-ecsf-02). Annex II 3.1(g) cites 'an implementing act under Article 19', but Article 19 in the Act (COM(2026) 502 final part 1) is titled 'Conformity self-assessment' (Union assurance level 1) — the substantively matching third-country adequacy mechanism is Article 18, 'Associated third countries'. Possible cross-reference inconsistency in the source draft; captured verbatim as printed, not corrected. Flag for Phase 3/owner verification against a later consolidated version of the proposal.

generalization_note.en: R8 structural_adaptation: the source text's third-country derogation mechanism (adopting an implementing act "under Article 19") has no clean {NATION}-level analog to substitute mechanically — it is described here functionally, as a formal adequacy/allowlist decision by the assessing government's own competent authority, mirroring the mechanism CADA's Article 18 ("Associated third countries") substantively describes. This record's own needs_review_note (set at extraction, Phase 2c) already documents that the verbatim text cites "Article 19" where Article 18 appears to be the intended provision — this generalization_note cites that existing documentation rather than repeating it, per this phase's instruction to cite "the erratum decision." generalization_class is set to structural_adaptation (not direct) for this record specifically, since the Article 18/19 mechanism, unlike the rest of this criterion's text, is not a mechanical rule substitution.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the technical and operational support or assistance related to the audited service, including subsequent sub-outsourcing arrangements, are initiated and performed exclusively within {TRUSTED_REGION}, by personnel that are Union residents, and by third parties that are not subject to the control of a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION}.

source_pointer.document: COM(2026) 502 final, 3.6.2026 — Annexes 1 to 3 (Cloud and AI Development Act proposal)

generalized_text.en: the audited provider demonstrates that the following software supply chain measures are in place: i. a complete and up-to-date SBOM and a list of identified dependencies relevant to the provision of the service are documented and made available to the auditing organisation; ii. measures in place to retain effective control over the software components or products by demonstrating that a outside {TRUSTED_REGION} or an entity established outside {TRUSTED_REGION} does not hold or exercise effective control over the design, development, maintenance, and evolution of those components or products. Effective control includes the ability to materially influence the technical evolution, maintenance priorities, security remediation, and long-term continuity of the component.

generalized_text.en: if the {GOVERNMENT_CUSTOMER} determines that imposing additional personnel screening and {TRUSTED_REGION} citizenship requirements are necessary, the audited provider should ensure that personnel meeting those requirements are available.

needs_review_note: source typo 'presonnel' preserved verbatim per project convention — erratum candidate

generalization_note.en: D-018 typo override: the verbatim source text (and source_text field) preserve the source PDF's own typo "presonnel" per D-018 (working rule 2 — verbatim discipline). generalized_text corrects this to "personnel": the generalized field is not a verbatim reproduction, so there is no reason to propagate a source typo into it; needs_review stays set on the record (D-018).
